1_discovery_sequence: 1d. Google Docs documentation inspection (BLOCKED — permission pending)
2_permission_sequence: 2a. Request Windows Beast SSH read access to /dev — highest priority (contains full Trinity apps)
2b. Request GitHub repository listing and read access — second priority
2c. Request Google Docs read access — third priority (docs supplement code)
2d. Each permission request is independent — can proceed in parallel
3_source_inspection_sequence: 3a. Windows Beast /dev: ls /dev, identify Trinity app directories, map package.json/tsconfig/routes/schema per app
3b. GitHub: gh repo list, identify projection repos, compare branches with local
3c. Google Docs: list docs by folder, extract titles/dates, read key product specs
3d. Each inspection produces a structured discovery report
4_canonicality_decision_points: 4a. For each projection: which source is canonical? (Operator decides, not UMH)
4b. /opt/OS/saas vs Windows /dev EOS — which is ahead? Which is canonical?
4c. Google Docs architecture claims vs actual code — which takes precedence?
4d. GitHub committed state vs local working state — which is truth?
4e. Schema v1 (integration) vs v2 (saas) — which survives?

5_eos_saas_vs_beast_comparison_plan.step_1: SSH to Beast, list /dev/eos or equivalent directory
5_eos_saas_vs_beast_comparison_plan.step_3: Compare database schema files (Drizzle config, schema.ts, migrations)
5_eos_saas_vs_beast_comparison_plan.step_6: Identify files in Beast not in VPS and vice versa

6_github_branch_repo_comparison_plan.step_2: For each projection-related repo: list branches, last commit dates
6_github_branch_repo_comparison_plan.step_3: Compare main branch source tree with /opt/OS local state
6_github_branch_repo_comparison_plan.step_4: Identify commits in remote not in local (and vice versa)
6_github_branch_repo_comparison_plan.step_5: Flag stale branches (>30 days no activity)

7_google_docs_claim_reconciliation_plan.step_1: List all docs in relevant Drive folders
7_google_docs_claim_reconciliation_plan.step_2: Categorize by projection (UMH, EOS, CreatorOS, LyfeOS, shared)
7_google_docs_claim_reconciliation_plan.step_3: Extract architecture/feature claims from key docs
7_google_docs_claim_reconciliation_plan.step_4: Cross-reference claims against actual code state
7_google_docs_claim_reconciliation_plan.step_5: Flag stale docs (claims that contradict current code)
7_google_docs_claim_reconciliation_plan.step_6: Flag aspirational docs (claims about unbuilt features)

8_automation_opportunities: Automated: file tree comparison between VPS and Beast (SSH + find)
Automated: GitHub repo listing and branch staleness check
Automated: Google Docs metadata extraction (titles, dates, folders)
Semi-automated: claim extraction from docs (AI-assisted with operator review)
9_requires_operator_approval: Source canonicality decisions for each projection
Schema version reconciliation direction (v1 vs v2)
File copy/sync from Beast to VPS (if needed)
10_must_never_be_automated: Cross-device file sync without explicit approval

11_proposed_canonical_source_architectur.EOS_backend: To be determined after Beast/GitHub inspection. Candidate: merge /opt/OS/saas + Beast source into single canonical location.
11_proposed_canonical_source_architectur.EOS_frontend: To be determined after Beast inspection. Likely on Beast at /dev.
11_proposed_canonical_source_architectur.CreatorOS: To be determined. Integration layer on VPS, full app likely on Beast.
11_proposed_canonical_source_architectur.LyfeOS: To be determined. Integration layer on VPS, full app likely on Beast.
11_proposed_canonical_source_architectur.shared_components: To be determined. May exist as shared packages on Beast or as standalone repos.

12_proposed_work_packets_for_next_phases: WP-003: Windows Beast /dev Trinity source inspection
WP-005: EOS Beast vs VPS convergence analysis

13_risk_register.risk: Beast source may be significantly ahead of VPS — large merge conflict potential
13_risk_register.mitigation: Compare before any sync. Use diff-first approach.

13_risk_register.risk: Google Docs may contain outdated architecture claims that mislead development
13_risk_register.mitigation: Cross-reference all claims against actual code state.

13_risk_register.risk: Schema v1/v2 split may require data migration
13_risk_register.mitigation: Assess migration complexity before choosing canonical schema.

13_risk_register.risk: Integration layer code duplication makes bugs harder to fix
13_risk_register.mitigation: Extract shared base before building new features.

13_risk_register.risk: Seed data contains instance context that could leak into production
13_risk_register.mitigation: Parametrize seed data before any deployment.

plan.14_rollback_strategy: No destructive operations in Phase 14.0. All inspection is read-only. Source registry and divergence data are additive. If any inspection produces incorrect results, the data files can be corrected without affecting source code.
plan.15_no_destructive_sync_guarantee: Phase 14.0 performs ZERO writes to external systems (GitHub, Google Docs, Windows Beast). All data generated is local to /opt/OS/data/umh/projection_reconciliation/. No source is auto-canonized. No files are copied, overwritten, or deleted.

what_was_inspected: /opt/OS/saas — 30 files, Hono backend, 22 tables, 12 route files, severe schema drift
projections/ — EOS (31 files), CreatorOS (8 files), LyfeOS (8 files)
GitHub — 4 repos found: OS, EntrepreneurOS, CreatorOS, LYFEOS — all Trinity apps are full-stack
what_was_blocked: Google Docs / Drive — no API credentials in CLI context
Windows Beast /dev — inspection agent still running (SSH confirmed accessible)
what_remains_uninspected: Windows Beast /dev Trinity app directories (pending agent completion)
Deep content comparison of GitHub repos vs VPS state

what_appears_canonical.EOS_candidate: GitHub EntrepreneurOS (full-stack, dormant) — strongest candidate for full app
what_appears_canonical.LyfeOS_candidate: GitHub LYFEOS (full-stack with tests, active May 20)

what_appears_partial.saas: /opt/OS/saas — server-only extract of EntrepreneurOS, severe schema drift
what_appears_partial.projections_eos: projections/eos — Python runtime layer, no frontend, v1 CRM schema

what_appears_stale.saas_schema: 7 orphan migration tables, 4 tables with no migration, broken journal
what_appears_stale.substrate_projections_refs: 16 substrate files with legacy EntrepreneurOS references

eos_saas_convergence_implications: saas/ is a server-side extract of GitHub EntrepreneurOS — missing client/ directory
saas/ has severe schema drift — migration journal only tracks 3 of 9 migrations
saas/ serves as unified API entrypoint (mounts both UMH and EOS routes)
Moving or restructuring saas/ would break UMH API routes
CRM tables (clients, transactions, etc.) use text org_id breaking RLS
Parallel Python/TypeScript type systems for same EOS domain

EntrepreneurOS is dormant — development migrated to OS/saas/
CreatorOS and LYFEOS are active (May 20 pushes)
62+ stale worktree-runtime-rs branches in OS repo need pruning

LyfeOS canonical source and priority (GitHub vs VPS skeleton)
